/en/javascript/2020/05/25/pixijs-v5-16-game-hp-bar/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (16) - Building a Game HP Bar
JavaScript
PixiJS
Canvas
w3HexSchool

Starting from the last post, our project finally started to feel a bit like an actual game. So in this one we'll build something every game needs — an HP bar. We'll draw it ourselves with two stacked rounded rectangles and a controllable width property to represent the health percentage.
/en/javascript/2020/05/18/pixijs-v5-tutorial-15-bump/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (15) - Collision Detection with Bump

Last time we learned how to draw geometric shapes in PIXI. Now it's time for something I find pretty interesting — collision detection. If you've ever Googled "PIXI hitTestRectangle" and hit a wall, this post explains why, and shows you a couple of ready-to-use functions you can drop straight into your project.
/en/javascript/2020/05/11/pixijs-v5-14-graphics-shapes/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (14) - Graphics (2): Circles, Polygons, Lines and Stars

Last time we covered basic geometry drawing in PixiJS, but only the rectangle. PIXI actually gives you a bunch of other shapes to draw too — circles, ellipses, rounded rectangles, polygons, lines and even stars. So this post walks through all the other geometry methods one by one.
/en/javascript/2020/03/30/pixijs-v5-08-ticker-2/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (8) - Ticker, Round 2: The Rest of the API

Last time we finally got an image moving. This one's a bit more relaxed — we just walk through the rest of the ticker API we used to move that image: add, addOnce, destroy, remove, start, stop, and update. Short and sweet.
/en/javascript/2020/03/16/pixijs-v5-06-texture-loader-shared/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (6) - Texture (2): A Second Way to Load Images

Last time we rendered images with new PIXI.Loader(). This post covers another way to do the same thing — PIXI.Loader.shared — and then we have some fun loading an image and spawning 25 copies of it on screen, laid out in a grid with anchors and a little positioning math.
/en/javascript/2020/03/09/pixijs-v5-tutorial-05-texture/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (5) - Texture: Getting Images onto the Canvas

If we can add text, we can add images too. PIXI uses the GPU and WebGL to render images, but an image has to be processed before you can actually use it — and a processed image is usually called a Texture. This post walks through loading images with PIXI.Texture and PIXI.Loader.
/en/javascript/2020/02/24/pixijs-v5-tutorial-03-container/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (3): Getting to Grips with Container

We've covered enough of the basics, so now it's time to look at one of the most important pieces of PixiJS — the Container. If you've ever used Bootstrap's grid system, the name will feel familiar, but Pixi's Container works a little differently. This post walks through adding children, reading them back, and dropping some text inside a container.
